name: Chocolate Espresso Torte for Passover
recipeInstructions:
NOTE: Margarine can be used if it is of good quality and parve, especially when serving a dairy meal.
Brewed espresso or very strong coffee should be used.
NOTE: For Passover, use Passover cake flour to dust the pans instead of regular flour.
Preheat oven to 325 degrees Fahrenheit. Grease a 9-inch spring-form pan.
Place wax paper on the bottom of the pan and grease and flour the sides; set aside.
Combine butter, sugar, and espresso in a double boiler and heat until the sugar dissolves. Pour the hot liquid over chocolate and stir until dissolved. Set aside.
Beat eggs and yolks until frothy and add to the chocolate mixture. Pour into the prepared pan.
Bake for one hour. The edges should crack slightly.
Remove from oven and let cool completely before covering and refrigerating.
Remove from the pan, sprinkle with confectioners sugar, and serve tiny thin slices. This rich cake has no flour and is almost as creamy as cheesecake. It must be refrigerated for at least 12 hours to set properly.
